ec_monitoring_program.primary_goal: Track salt accumulation before visible turf decline becomes severe.
core_principles: Trend EC over time instead of treating one reading as the whole story.
Interpret EC alongside moisture, bicarbonates, sodium hazard, and turf response.
Use the same sampling depth and method consistently.

benchmark_ranges.sampling_style: Routine in-season checks where salt accumulation is plausible
benchmark_ranges.decision_rule: Use rising trends plus visible stress, not just color, to trigger correction

cautions: Localized high EC can exist even when average area readings look acceptable.
Do not confuse wilt from salts with ordinary drought stress.

leaching_program_for_salts.primary_goal: Move accumulated salts below the primary rootzone without creating chronic saturation.
suitable_sites: fairways using poorer quality irrigation water
core_principles: Leaching works best when drainage and infiltration actually allow salts to move.
Plan leaching around weather, drainage capacity, and follow-up moisture management.
Use enough water to move salts, not just wet the surface.

benchmark_ranges.fit: Best used when EC is elevated and drainage is adequate enough to flush the profile

cautions: Leaching on a poorly drained profile can worsen anaerobic stress.
Do not assume one flush fixes an ongoing water-quality problem.
Re-check EC after the program instead of assuming success.

sodium_hazard_and_structure_loss.primary_goal: Recognize when infiltration and surface sealing are tied to sodium and poor water chemistry, not just compaction.
core_principles: SAR and sodium effects show up in infiltration, crusting, and sealing as much as in leaf symptoms.
Chemistry correction and physical management usually need to happen together.
Water quality should be evaluated as part of the rootzone program.

benchmark_ranges.decision_rule: If infiltration keeps collapsing despite cultivation, review sodium and bicarbonate pressure

cautions: Do not blame every slow surface on compaction alone.
Gypsum or acidification only helps when the chemistry and drainage pathway fit the recommendation.
Surface symptoms may lag behind structural decline.

reclaimed_water_management.primary_goal: Use lower-quality irrigation water without letting salts and bicarbonates quietly erode turf performance.
suitable_sites: sports fields on municipal reuse water
core_principles: Reclaimed-water management is a season-long monitoring program, not a one-time adjustment.
Salt load, bicarbonates, and sodium risk all matter.
Watch species tolerance and site drainage together.

benchmark_ranges.program_style: Routine water tests and periodic soil/EC checks through the irrigation season

cautions: Warm-season turf may tolerate conditions that cool-season turf will not.
Localized stress often appears first on edges, mounds, and poorly flushed areas.
Nutrient contribution from reclaimed water should still be accounted for in the fertility plan.

gypsum_and_amendment_decision_tree.primary_goal: Use calcium-based amendments only when the soil or water chemistry problem actually supports them.
suitable_sites: sites with structure loss and chemistry documentation
core_principles: Base amendment choice on water and soil chemistry, not habit.
Amendments need a pathway for displaced salts to leave the profile.
Physical and chemical corrections often need to be paired.

benchmark_ranges.decision_rule: Documented sodium or related chemistry issues should drive the recommendation

cautions: Gypsum is not a cure-all for every infiltration problem.
Without drainage and leaching, amendments may not deliver the intended benefit.
Track outcome after application instead of assuming chemistry is fixed.

salt_stress_diagnostics.primary_goal: Differentiate salinity stress from drought, fertility deficiency, and root disease.
suitable_sites: areas with recurring edge burn or uneven wilt
core_principles: Salt stress often overlaps with moisture stress but does not behave the same way.
Check EC, water quality, and symptom distribution before guessing.
Look for site and zone patterns tied to irrigation water delivery.

benchmark_ranges.first_response: Probe moisture and test EC before escalating to chemistry or pesticide conclusions

cautions: A wilted plant in salty soil is not always under-watered.
Leaf edge burn alone does not identify the whole cause.
Do not skip root inspection when stress is severe.
